verses.latin_vulgate: Mane autem facto, consilium inierunt omnes principes sacerdotum et seniores populi adversus Jesum, ut eum morti traderent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρωΐας δὲ γενομένης συμβούλιον ἔλαβον πάντες οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι τοῦ λαοῦ κατὰ τοῦ ἰησοῦ ὥστε θανατῶσαι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: When the morning was come, all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vinctum adduxerunt eum, et tradiderunt Pontio Pilato præsidi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ δήσαντες αὐτὸν ἀπήγαγον καὶ παρέδωκαν αὐτὸν ποντίῳ πιλάτῳ τῷ ἡγεμόνι
verses.king_james: And when they had bound him, they led *him* away, and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc videns Judas, qui eum tradidit, quod damnatus esset, pœnitentia ductus, retulit triginta argenteos principibus sacerdotum, et senioribus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἰδὼν ἰούδας ὁ παραδιδοὺς αὐτὸν ὅτι κατεκρίθη μεταμεληθεὶς ἀπέστρεψεν τὰ τριάκοντα ἀργύρια τοῖς ἀρχιερεῦσιν καὶ τοῖς πρεσβυτέροις
verses.king_james: Then Judas, which had betrayed him, when he saw that he was condemned, repented himself, and brought again the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Peccavi, tradens sanguinem justum. At illi dixerunt: Quid ad nos? tu videris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων Ἥμαρτον παραδοὺς αἷμα ἀθῷον οἱ δὲ εἶπον τί πρὸς ἡμᾶς σὺ ὄψει
verses.king_james: Saying, I have sinned in that I have betrayed the innocent blood. And they said, What *is that* to us? see thou *to that*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et projectis argenteis in templo, recessit: et abiens laqueo se suspendit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ῥίψας τὰ ἀργύρια ἕν τῷ ναῷ ἀνεχώρησεν καὶ ἀπελθὼν ἀπήγξατο
verses.king_james: And he cast down the pieces of silver in the temple, and departed, and went and hanged himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes autem sacerdotum, acceptis argenteis, dixerunt: Non licet eos mittere in corbonam: quia pretium sanguinis est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς λαβόντες τὰ ἀργύρια εἶπον οὐκ ἔξεστιν βαλεῖν αὐτὰ εἰς τὸν κορβανᾶν ἐπεὶ τιμὴ αἵματός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And the chief priests took the silver pieces, and said, It is not lawful for to put them into the treasury, because it is the price of blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consilio autem inito, emerunt ex illis agrum figuli, in sepulturam peregrinorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συμβούλιον δὲ λαβόντες ἠγόρασαν ἐξ αὐτῶν τὸν ἀγρὸν τοῦ κεραμέως εἰς ταφὴν τοῖς ξένοις
verses.king_james: And they took counsel, and bought with them the potter's field, to bury strangers in.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hoc vocatus est ager ille, Haceldama, hoc est, Ager sanguinis, usque in hodiernum diem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ ἐκλήθη ὁ ἀγρὸς ἐκεῖνος ἀγρὸς αἵματος ἕως τῆς σήμερον
verses.king_james: Wherefore that field was called, The field of blood, unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc impletum est quod dictum est per Jeremiam prophetam, dicentem: Et acceperunt triginta argenteos pretium appretiati, quem appretiaverunt a filiis Israël:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἐπληρώθη τὸ ῥηθὲν διὰ ἰερεμίου τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος καὶ ἔλαβον τὰ τριάκοντα ἀργύρια τὴν τιμὴν τοῦ τετιμημένου ὃν ἐτιμήσαντο ἀπὸ υἱῶν ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the prophet, saying, And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him that was valued, whom they of the children of Israel did value;

verses.latin_vulgate: et dederunt eos in agrum figuli, sicut constituit mihi Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔδωκαν αὐτὰ εἰς τὸν ἀγρὸν τοῦ κεραμέως καθὰ συνέταξέν μοι κύριος
verses.king_james: And gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord appointed me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem stetit ante præsidem, et interrogavit eum præses, dicens: Tu es rex Judæorum? Dicit illi Jesus: Tu dicis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἔστη ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ ἡγεμόνος καὶ ἐπηρώτησεν αὐτὸν ὁ ἡγεμὼν λέγων σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν ἰουδαίων ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἔφη αὐτῷ σὺ λέγεις
verses.king_james: And Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said unto him, Thou sayest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum accusaretur a principibus sacerdotum et senioribus, nihil respondit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τῷ κατηγορεῖσθαι αὐτὸν ὑπὸ τῶν ἀρχιερέων καὶ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων οὐδὲν ἀπεκρίνατο
verses.king_james: And when he was accused of the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicit illi Pilatus: Non audis quanta adversum te dicunt testimonia?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ πιλᾶτος οὐκ ἀκούεις πόσα σου καταμαρτυροῦσιν
verses.king_james: Then said Pilate unto him, Hearest thou not how many things they witness against thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non respondit ei ad ullum verbum, ita ut miraretur præses vehementer.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ πρὸς οὐδὲ ἓν ῥῆμα ὥστε θαυμάζειν τὸν ἡγεμόνα λίαν
verses.king_james: And he answered him to never a word; insomuch that the governor marvelled greatly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per diem autem solemnem consueverat præses populo dimittere unum vinctum, quem voluissent:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ δὲ ἑορτὴν εἰώθει ὁ ἡγεμὼν ἀπολύειν ἕνα τῷ ὄχλῳ δέσμιον ὃν ἤθελον
verses.king_james: Now at *that* feast the governor was wont to release unto the people a prisoner, whom they would.

verses.latin_vulgate: habebat autem tunc vinctum insignem, qui dicebatur Barrabas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶχον δὲ τότε δέσμιον ἐπίσημον λεγόμενον βαραββᾶν
verses.king_james: And they had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregatis ergo illis, dixit Pilatus: Quem vultis dimittam vobis: Barabbam, an Jesum, qui dicitur Christus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνηγμένων οὖν αὐτῶν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ πιλᾶτος τίνα θέλετε ἀπολύσω ὑμῖν βαραββᾶν ἢ ἰησοῦν τὸν λεγόμενον χριστόν
verses.king_james: Therefore when they were gathered together, Pilate said unto them, Whom will ye that I release unto you? Barabbas, or Jesus which is called Christ?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sciebat enim quod per invidiam tradidissent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾔδει γὰρ ὅτι διὰ φθόνον παρέδωκαν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: For he knew that for envy they had delivered him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sedente autem illo pro tribunali, misit ad eum uxor ejus, dicens: Nihil tibi, et justo illi: multa enim passa sum hodie per visum propter eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθημένου δὲ αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τοῦ βήματος ἀπέστειλεν πρὸς αὐτὸν ἡ γυνὴ αὐτοῦ λέγουσα μηδὲν σοὶ καὶ τῷ δικαίῳ ἐκείνῳ πολλὰ γὰρ ἔπαθον σήμερον κατ᾽ ὄναρ δι᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: When he was set down on the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes autem sacerdotum et seniores persuaserunt populis ut peterent Barabbam, Jesum vero perderent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι ἔπεισαν τοὺς ὄχλους ἵνα αἰτήσωνται τὸν βαραββᾶν τὸν δὲ ἰησοῦν ἀπολέσωσιν
verses.king_james: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitude that they should ask Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem præses, ait illis: Quem vultis vobis de duobus dimitti? At illi dixerunt: Barabbam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἡγεμὼν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς τίνα θέλετε ἀπὸ τῶν δύο ἀπολύσω ὑμῖν οἱ δὲ εἰπον βαραββᾶν
verses.king_james: The governor answered and said unto them, Whether of the twain will ye that I release unto you? They said, Barabbas.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit illis Pilatus: Quid igitur faciam de Jesu, qui dicitur Christus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ πιλᾶτος τί οὐν ποιήσω ἰησοῦν τὸν λεγόμενον χριστόν λέγουσιν αὐτῷ πάντες σταυρωθήτω
verses.king_james: Pilate saith unto them, What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ? *They* all say unto him, Let him be crucified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt omnes: Crucifigatur. Ait illis præses: Quid enim mali fecit? At illi magis clamabant dicentes: Crucifigatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἡγεμὼν ἔφη τί γὰρ κακὸν ἐποίησεν οἱ δὲ περισσῶς ἔκραζον λέγοντες σταυρωθήτω
verses.king_james: And the governor said, Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying, Let him be crucified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem Pilatus quia nihil proficeret, sed magis tumultus fieret: accepta aqua, lavit manus coram populo, dicens: Innocens ego sum a sanguine justi hujus: vos videritis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ πιλᾶτος ὅτι οὐδὲν ὠφελεῖ ἀλλὰ μᾶλλον θόρυβος γίνεται λαβὼν ὕδωρ ἀπενίψατο τὰς χεῖρας ἀπέναντι τοῦ ὄχλου λέγων ἀθῷός εἰμι ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ δικαίου τούτου ὑμεῖς ὄψεσθε
verses.king_james: When Pilate saw that he could prevail nothing, but *that* rather a tumult was made, he took water, and washed *his* hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this just person: see ye *to it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens universus populus, dixit: Sanguis ejus super nos, et super filios nostros.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς πᾶς ὁ λαὸς εἶπεν τὸ αἷμα αὐτοῦ ἐφ᾽ ἡμᾶς καὶ ἐπὶ τὰ τέκνα ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Then answered all the people, and said, His blood *be* on us, and on our children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dimisit illis Barabbam: Jesum autem flagellatum tradidit eis ut crucifigeretur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἀπέλυσεν αὐτοῖς τὸν βαραββᾶν τὸν δὲ ἰησοῦν φραγελλώσας παρέδωκεν ἵνα σταυρωθῇ
verses.king_james: Then released he Barabbas unto them: and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered *him* to be crucified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc milites præsidis suscipientes Jesum in prætorium, congregaverunt ad eum universam cohortem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε οἱ στρατιῶται τοῦ ἡγεμόνος παραλαβόντες τὸν ἰησοῦν εἰς τὸ πραιτώριον συνήγαγον ἐπ᾽ αὐτὸν ὅλην τὴν σπεῖραν
verses.king_james: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the common hall, and gathered unto him the whole band *of soldiers*.

verses.latin_vulgate: et exeuntes eum, chlamydem coccineam circumdederunt ei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκδύσαντες αὐτὸν περιέθηκαν αὐτῷ χλαμύδα κοκκίνην
verses.king_james: And they stripped him, and put on him a scarlet robe.

verses.latin_vulgate: et plectentes coronam de spinis, posuerunt super caput ejus, et arundinem in dextera ejus. Et genu flexo ante eum, illudebant ei, dicentes: Ave rex Judæorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πλέξαντες στέφανον ἐξ ἀκανθῶν ἐπέθηκαν ἐπὶ τὴν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ καὶ κάλαμον ἐπὶ τὴν δεξιὰν αὐτοῦ καὶ γονυπετήσαντες ἔμπροσθεν αὐτοῦ ἐνέπαιζον αὐτῷ λέγοντες χαῖρε ὁ βασιλεῦς τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put *it* upon his head, and a reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exspuentes in eum, acceperunt arundinem, et percutiebant caput ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμπτύσαντες εἰς αὐτὸν ἔλαβον τὸν κάλαμον καὶ ἔτυπτον εἰς τὴν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And they spit upon him, and took the reed, and smote him on the head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et postquam illuserunt ei, exuerunt eum chlamyde, et induerunt eum vestimentis ejus, et duxerunt eum ut crucifigerent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε ἐνέπαιξαν αὐτῷ ἐξέδυσαν αὐτὸν τὴν χλαμύδα καὶ ἐνέδυσαν αὐτὸν τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀπήγαγον αὐτὸν εἰς τὸ σταυρῶσαι
verses.king_james: And after that they had mocked him, they took the robe off from him, and put his own raiment on him, and led him away to crucify *him*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exeuntes autem invenerunt hominem Cyrenæum, nomine Simonem: hunc angariaverunt ut tolleret crucem ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξερχόμενοι δὲ εὗρον ἄνθρωπον κυρηναῖον ὀνόματι σίμωνα τοῦτον ἠγγάρευσαν ἵνα ἄρῃ τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to bear his cross.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venerunt in locum qui dicitur Golgotha, quod est Calvariæ locus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθόντες εἰς τόπον λεγόμενον γολγοθᾶ ὅς ἐστιν λεγόμενος κρανίου τόπος
verses.king_james: And when they were come unto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of a skull,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dederunt ei vinum bibere cum felle mistum. Et cum gustasset, noluit bibere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδωκαν αὐτῷ πιεῖν ὄξος μετὰ χολῆς μεμιγμένον καὶ γευσάμενος οὐκ ἤθελεν πιεῖν
verses.king_james: They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted *thereof*, he would not drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam autem crucifixerunt eum, diviserunt vestimenta ejus, sortem mittentes: ut impleretur quod dictum est per prophetam dicentem: Diviserunt sibi vestimenta mea, et super vestem meam miserunt sortem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σταυρώσαντες δὲ αὐτὸν διεμερίσαντο τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ βάλλοντες κλῆρον ἵνα πληρωθῇ τὸ ῥηθὲν ὑπὸ τοῦ προφήτου διεμερίσαντο τὰ ἱμάτια μου ἑαυτοῖς καὶ ἐπὶ τὸν ἱματισμόν μου ἔβαλον κλῆρον
verses.king_james: And they crucified him, and parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, They parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sedentes servabant eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καθήμενοι ἐτήρουν αὐτὸν ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: And sitting down they watched him there;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et imposuerunt super caput ejus causam ipsius scriptam: Hic est Jesus rex Judæorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπέθηκαν ἐπάνω τῆς κεφαλῆς αὐτοῦ τὴν αἰτίαν αὐτοῦ γεγραμμένην οὗτός ἐστιν ἰησοῦς ὁ βασιλεῦς τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: And set up over his head his accusation written, THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc crucifixi sunt cum eo duo latrones: unus a dextris, et unus a sinistris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε σταυροῦνται σὺν αὐτῷ δύο λῃσταί εἷς ἐκ δεξιῶν καὶ εἷς ἐξ εὐωνύμων
verses.king_james: Then were there two thieves crucified with him, one on the right hand, and another on the left.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prætereuntes autem blasphemabant eum moventes capita sua,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ παραπορευόμενοι ἐβλασφήμουν αὐτὸν κινοῦντες τὰς κεφαλὰς αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And they that passed by reviled him, wagging their heads,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dicentes: Vah! qui destruis templum Dei, et in triduo illud reædificas: salva temetipsum: si Filius Dei es, descende de cruce.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγοντες ὁ καταλύων τὸν ναὸν καὶ ἐν τρισὶν ἡμέραις οἰκοδομῶν σῶσον σεαυτόν εἰ υἱὸς εἶ τοῦ θεοῦ κατάβηθι ἀπὸ τοῦ σταυροῦ
verses.king_james: And saying, Thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest *it* in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.

verses.latin_vulgate: Similiter et principes sacerdotum illudentes cum scribis et senioribus dicebant:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμοίως δὲ καὶ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς ἐμπαίζοντες μετὰ τῶν γραμματέων καὶ πρεσβυτέρων ἔλεγον
verses.king_james: Likewise also the chief priests mocking *him*, with the scribes and elders, said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Alios salvos fecit, seipsum non potest salvum facere: si rex Israël est, descendat nunc de cruce, et credimus ei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλους ἔσωσεν ἑαυτὸν οὐ δύναται σῶσαι εἰ βασιλεὺς ἰσραήλ ἐστιν καταβάτω νῦν ἀπὸ τοῦ σταυροῦ καὶ πιστεύσομεν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: He saved others; himself he cannot save. If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him.

verses.latin_vulgate: confidit in Deo: liberet nunc, si vult eum: dixit enim: Quia Filius Dei sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέποιθεν ἐπὶ τὸν θεόν ῥυσάσθω νῦν αὐτόν εἰ θέλει αὐτόν εἶπεν γὰρ ὅτι θεοῦ εἰμι υἱός
verses.king_james: He trusted in God; let him deliver him now, if he will have him: for he said, I am the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Idipsum autem et latrones, qui crucifixi erant cum eo, improperabant ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δ᾽ αὐτὸ καὶ οἱ λῃσταὶ οἱ συσταυρωθέντες αὐτῷ ὠνείδιζον αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: The thieves also, which were crucified with him, cast the same in his teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: A sexta autem hora tenebræ factæ sunt super universam terram usque ad horam nonam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ δὲ ἕκτης ὥρας σκότος ἐγένετο ἐπὶ πᾶσαν τὴν γῆν ἕως ὥρας ἐννάτης
verses.king_james: Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et circa horam nonam clamavit Jesus voce magna, dicens: Eli, Eli, lamma sabacthani? hoc est: Deus meus, Deus meus, ut quid dereliquisti me?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ τὴν ἐννάτην ὥραν ἀνεβόησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς φωνῇ μεγάλῃ λέγων ηλι ηλι λαμὰ σαβαχθανι τοῦτ᾽ ἔστιν φεέ μου θεέ μου ἱνατί με ἐγκατέλιπες
verses.king_james: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam autem illic stantes, et audientes, dicebant: Eliam vocat iste.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τινὲς δὲ τῶν ἐκεῖ ἑστώτων ἀκούσαντες ἔλεγον ὅτι ἠλίαν φωνεῖ οὗτος
verses.king_james: Some of them that stood there, when they heard *that*, said, This *man* calleth for Elias.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et continuo currens unus ex eis, acceptam spongiam implevit aceto, et imposuit arundini, et dabat ei bibere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως δραμὼν εἷς ἐξ αὐτῶν καὶ λαβὼν σπόγγον πλήσας τε ὄξους καὶ περιθεὶς καλάμῳ ἐπότιζεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And straightway one of them ran, and took a spunge, and filled *it* with vinegar, and put *it* on a reed, and gave him to drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ceteri vero dicebant: Sine, videamus an veniat Elias liberans eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ λοιποὶ ἔλεγον ἄφες ἴδωμεν εἰ ἔρχεται ἠλίας σώσων αὐτόν
verses.king_james: The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem iterum clamans voce magna, emisit spiritum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς πάλιν κράξας φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ἀφῆκεν τὸ πνεῦμα
verses.king_james: Jesus, when he had cried again with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce velum templi scissum est in duas partes a summo usque deorsum: et terra mota est, et petræ scissæ sunt,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού τὸ καταπέτασμα τοῦ ναοῦ ἐσχίσθη εἰς δύο ἀπὸ ἄνωθεν ἕως κάτω καὶ ἡ γῆ ἐσείσθη καὶ αἱ πέτραι ἐσχίσθησαν
verses.king_james: And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent;

verses.latin_vulgate: et monumenta aperta sunt: et multa corpora sanctorum, qui dormierant, surrexerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὰ μνημεῖα ἀνεῴχθησαν καὶ πολλὰ σώματα τῶν κεκοιμημένων ἁγίων ἠγέρθη
verses.king_james: And the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exeuntes de monumentis post resurrectionem ejus, venerunt in sanctam civitatem, et apparuerunt multis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξελθόντες ἐκ τῶν μνημείων μετὰ τὴν ἔγερσιν αὐτοῦ εἰσῆλθον εἰς τὴν ἁγίαν πόλιν καὶ ἐνεφανίσθησαν πολλοῖς
verses.king_james: And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many.

verses.latin_vulgate: Centurio autem, et qui cum eo erant, custodientes Jesum, viso terræmotu, et his quæ fiebant, timuerunt valde, dicentes: Vere Filius Dei erat iste.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἑκατόνταρχος καὶ οἱ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ τηροῦντες τὸν ἰησοῦν ἰδόντες τὸν σεισμὸν καὶ τὰ γενόμενα ἐφοβήθησαν σφόδρα λέγοντες ἀληθῶς θεοῦ υἱὸς ἦν οὗτος
verses.king_james: Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem ibi mulieres multæ a longe, quæ secutæ erant Jesum a Galilæa, ministrantes ei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ ἐκεῖ γυναῖκες πολλαὶ ἀπὸ μακρόθεν θεωροῦσαι αἵτινες ἠκολούθησαν τῷ ἰησοῦ ἀπὸ τῆς γαλιλαίας διακονοῦσαι αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And many women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:

verses.latin_vulgate: inter quas erat Maria Magdalene, et Maria Jacobi, et Joseph mater, et mater filiorum Zebedæi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν αἷς ἦν μαρία ἡ μαγδαληνὴ καὶ μαρία ἡ τοῦ ἰακώβου καὶ ἰωσὴ μήτηρ καὶ ἡ μήτηρ τῶν υἱῶν ζεβεδαίου
verses.king_james: Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem sero factum esset, venit quidam homo dives ab Arimathæa, nomine Joseph, qui et ipse discipulus erat Jesu:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης ἦλθεν ἄνθρωπος πλούσιος ἀπὸ ἁριμαθαίας τοὔνομα ἰωσήφ ὃς καὶ αὐτὸς ἐμαθήτευσεν τῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathaea, named Joseph, who also himself was Jesus' disciple:

verses.latin_vulgate: hic accessit ad Pilatum, et petiit corpus Jesu. Tunc Pilatus jussit reddi corpus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος προσελθὼν τῷ πιλάτῳ ᾐτήσατο τὸ σῶμα τοῦ ἰησοῦ τότε ὁ πιλᾶτος ἐκέλευσεν ἀποδοθῆναι τὸ σῶμα
verses.king_james: He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accepto corpore, Joseph involvit illud in sindone munda,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λαβὼν τὸ σῶμα ὁ ἰωσὴφ ἐνετύλιξεν αὐτὸ σινδόνι καθαρᾷ
verses.king_james: And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,

verses.latin_vulgate: et posuit illud in monumento suo novo, quod exciderat in petra. Et advolvit saxum magnum ad ostium monumenti, et abiit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔθηκεν αὐτὸ ἐν τῷ καινῷ αὐτοῦ μνημείῳ ὃ ἐλατόμησεν ἐν τῇ πέτρᾳ καὶ προσκυλίσας λίθον μέγαν τῇ θύρᾳ τοῦ μνημείου ἀπῆλθεν
verses.king_james: And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre, and departed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem ibi Maria Magdalene, et altera Maria, sedentes contra sepulchrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἐκεῖ μαρία ἡ μαγδαληνὴ καὶ ἡ ἄλλη μαριά καθήμεναι ἀπέναντι τοῦ τάφου
verses.king_james: And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.

verses.latin_vulgate: Altera autem die, quæ est post Parasceven, convenerunt principes sacerdotum et pharisæi ad Pilatum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ δὲ ἐπαύριον ἥτις ἐστὶν μετὰ τὴν παρασκευήν συνήχθησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι πρὸς πιλᾶτον
verses.king_james: Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Domine, recordati sumus, quia seductor ille dixit adhuc vivens: Post tres dies resurgam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες κύριε ἐμνήσθημεν ὅτι ἐκεῖνος ὁ πλάνος εἶπεν ἔτι ζῶν μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας ἐγείρομαι
verses.king_james: Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jube ergo custodiri sepulchrum usque in diem tertium: ne forte veniant discipuli ejus, et furentur eum, et dicant plebi: Surrexit a mortuis: et erit novissimus error pejor priore.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κέλευσον οὖν ἀσφαλισθῆναι τὸν τάφον ἕως τῆς τρίτης ἡμέρας μήποτε ἐλθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ νυκτὸς κλέψωσιν αὐτὸν καὶ εἴπωσιν τῷ λαῷ ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν καὶ ἔσται ἡ ἐσχάτη πλάνη χείρων τῆς πρώτης
verses.king_james: Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error shall be worse than the first.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illis Pilatus: Habetis custodiam, ite, custodite sicut scitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφη δὲ αὐτοῖς ὁ πιλᾶτος ἕχετε κουστωδίαν ὑπάγετε ἀσφαλίσασθε ὡς οἴδατε
verses.king_james: Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make *it* as sure as ye can.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi autem abeuntes, munierunt sepulchrum, signantes lapidem, cum custodibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ πορευθέντες ἠσφαλίσαντο τὸν τάφον σφραγίσαντες τὸν λίθον μετὰ τῆς κουστωδίας
verses.king_james: So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.
